# Tinybox SDK

---
title: Create a bucket
url: https://example.com/api/buckets/create/
type: reference
summary: Provision a new storage bucket in the workspace.
---

Request

```bash
curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' \
  -d '{"name":"my-bucket","region":"us-east-1"}' \
  https://api.tinybox.dev/v1/buckets
```

Response

The API returns the created bucket descriptor. See List buckets.


---
title: Delete a bucket
url: https://example.com/api/buckets/delete/
type: reference
summary: Remove an empty bucket from the workspace.
---

DELETE /v1/buckets/{name} — fails with 409 when the bucket contains objects.


---
title: Get a bucket
url: https://example.com/api/buckets/get/
type: reference
summary: Fetch a single bucket descriptor by name.
---

GET /v1/buckets/{name} — returns the bucket descriptor or 404 when absent.


---
title: List buckets
url: https://example.com/api/buckets/list/
type: reference
summary: List all buckets visible to the calling principal.
---

GET /v1/buckets — paginates via ?cursor= query parameter; default page size 100.


---
title: Delete an object
url: https://example.com/api/objects/delete/
type: reference
summary: Remove an object key from a bucket.
---

DELETE /v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key} — soft-deletes when versioning is on.


---
title: Download an object
url: https://example.com/api/objects/download/
type: reference
summary: Fetch an object payload, optionally honoring a version selector.
---

GET /v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key} — supports ?version= and HTTP range requests.


---
title: List objects
url: https://example.com/api/objects/list/
type: reference
summary: Enumerate objects in a bucket with optional prefix filtering.
---

GET /v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects — ?prefix= and ?cursor= query parameters.


---
title: Upload an object
url: https://example.com/api/objects/upload/
type: reference
summary: Stream a payload into a bucket under a chosen key.
---

PUT /v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects/{key} — Content-Type and Content-Length are required.


---
title: Delete a webhook
url: https://example.com/api/webhooks/delete/
type: reference
summary: Remove a webhook subscription by id.
---

DELETE /v1/webhooks/{id} — idempotent; 404 when the subscription is already gone.


---
title: List webhooks
url: https://example.com/api/webhooks/list/
type: reference
summary: Enumerate webhook subscriptions for the current project.
---

GET /v1/webhooks — returns subscription records ordered by created_at descending.


---
title: Register a webhook
url: https://example.com/api/webhooks/register/
type: reference
summary: Subscribe a callback URL to bucket or object lifecycle events.
---

POST /v1/webhooks — body declares target_url, event_types, and bucket filter.


---
title: Changelog
url: https://example.com/changelog/
type: reference
summary: Release notes for the Tinybox SDK reference docs.
---

v0.1 — 2026-05-02

Initial public release.Buckets, Objects, and Webhooks API surfaces.100+ recipes and 60+ troubleshooting entries.


---
title: Authentication
url: https://example.com/concepts/auth/
type: doc
summary: Bearer-token authentication and per-project scoping.
---

Authenticate via a bearer token in the Authorization header.
Tokens are issued from the dashboard and scoped per project.


---
title: Data model
url: https://example.com/concepts/data-model/
type: doc
summary: "Buckets, Objects, and Webhooks: the three top-level resources."
---

Tinybox exposes three nouns: Buckets (containers), Objects
(blobs), and Webhooks (lifecycle event delivery). Buckets nest objects;
webhooks subscribe to events on either.


---
title: Buckets concept
url: https://example.com/concepts/storage/buckets/
type: doc
summary: Buckets are regional containers that hold objects.
---

Buckets are the unit of regional placement and access policy. Each
bucket lives in exactly one region; cross-region replication is opt-in.


---
title: Lifecycle policies
url: https://example.com/concepts/storage/lifecycle/
type: doc
summary: Automatic transitions between storage classes by age or tag.
---

Lifecycle policies move objects between storage classes (STANDARD,
INFREQUENT, ARCHIVE, DEEP) based on age or tag predicates.


---
title: Objects concept
url: https://example.com/concepts/storage/objects/
type: doc
summary: Objects are immutable byte sequences keyed within a bucket.
---

Object keys are UTF-8 strings up to 1024 bytes. Object payloads are
immutable; an upload to an existing key creates a new version when the
bucket has versioning enabled.


---
title: Your first project
url: https://example.com/getting-started/first-project/
type: doc
summary: Walk through wiring a real application against Tinybox storage.
---

A guided walkthrough that wires the SDK into an Express app and uploads
user-supplied media to a fresh bucket. End-to-end in roughly 100 lines.


---
title: Install the SDK
url: https://example.com/getting-started/install/
type: doc
summary: Install the Tinybox SDK via the package manager of your choice.
---

Install

```bash
npm install @tinybox/sdk
```

The SDK targets Node 20+ and modern browsers. ESM only.


---
title: Quickstart
url: https://example.com/getting-started/quickstart/
type: doc
summary: Provision a bucket and upload your first object in under a minute.
---

Five minutes

```ts
import { Tinybox } from '@tinybox/sdk';
const tb = new Tinybox({ token: process.env.TINYBOX_TOKEN });
await tb.buckets.create({ name: 'my-bucket', region: 'us-east-1' });
```


---
title: Tinybox SDK overview
url: https://example.com/intro/
type: doc
summary: "Introduction to the Tinybox storage SDK: data model, capabilities, and a tour of the docs."
---

Tinybox is a fictional object-storage product used as the corpus for the PRD-701 conformance example.
The docs are organised into Getting Started, Concepts, an API reference grouped by surface area, recipes,
and a troubleshooting catalog. Each page is one ACT node per PRD-201; each sidebars.js category becomes a
synthesized parent node per PRD-404-R6.

Where to start

Start with Getting started or jump to the API reference.


---
title: Archive audit trail cli (Archival)
url: https://example.com/recipes/archival/archive-audit-trail-cli/
type: recipe
summary: Archive audit trail cli — archival workflow walkthrough.
---

Goal

Demonstrate "archive audit trail (cli)" against the Tinybox archival surface end to end.

Walkthrough

```bash
# Step 1 — prepare a bucket for the archival workflow.
tinybox buckets create my-bucket --region us-east-1
```

Apply the recipe; verify with the API reference under API reference.

---
title: Content md5 verification overview (Ingestion)
url: https://example.com/recipes/ingestion/content-md5-verification-overview/
type: recipe
summary: Content md5 verification overview — ingestion workflow walkthrough.
---

Goal

Demonstrate "content md5 verification (overview)" against the Tinybox ingestion surface end to end.

Walkthrough

```bash
# Step 1 — prepare a bucket for the ingestion workflow.
tinybox buckets create my-bucket --region us-east-1
```

Apply the recipe; verify with the API reference under API reference.

---
title: Bucket lifecycle policy reference
url: https://example.com/recipes/lifecycle-policy/
type: reference
summary: Lifecycle rules table for retention configuration.
---

The supported lifecycle transitions:

```json
{
  "transitions": [
    { "from": "STANDARD", "to": "INFREQUENT", "after_days": 30 },
    { "from": "INFREQUENT", "to": "ARCHIVE",   "after_days": 90 },
    { "from": "ARCHIVE",   "to": "DEEP",       "after_days": 365 }
  ]
}
```

Apply the policy with PUT /v1/buckets/{bucket}/lifecycle.

---

Goal

Demonstrate "reduce listing cost (overview)" against the Tinybox performance surface end to end.

Walkthrough

```bash
# Step 1 — prepare a bucket for the performance workflow.
tinybox buckets create my-bucket --region us-east-1
```

Apply the recipe; verify with the API reference under API reference.

---
title: Audit log missing reproduction (Auth)
url: https://example.com/troubleshooting/auth/audit-log-missing-reproduction/
type: troubleshooting
summary: "Resolve \"audit log missing\" — reproduction for the Auth surface."
---

Symptom

An operation against the Auth surface fails with an error matching "audit log missing (reproduction)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Auth API reference for the exact field set.


---
title: Audit log missing resolution (Auth)
url: https://example.com/troubleshooting/auth/audit-log-missing-resolution/
type: troubleshooting
summary: "Resolve \"audit log missing\" — resolution for the Auth surface."
---

Symptom

An operation against the Auth surface fails with an error matching "audit log missing (resolution)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Auth API reference for the exact field set.


---

url: https://example.com/troubleshooting/auth/audit-log-missing-symptom/
type: troubleshooting
summary: "Resolve \"audit log missing\" — symptom for the Auth surface."
---

An operation against the Auth surface fails with an error matching "audit log missing (symptom)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Auth API reference for the exact field set.

---
title: Cross project token resolution (Auth)
url: https://example.com/troubleshooting/auth/cross-project-token-resolution/
type: troubleshooting
summary: "Resolve \"cross project token\" — resolution for the Auth surface."
---

Symptom

An operation against the Auth surface fails with an error matching "cross project token (resolution)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Auth API reference for the exact field set.


---
title: Cross project token symptom (Auth)
url: https://example.com/troubleshooting/auth/cross-project-token-symptom/
type: troubleshooting
summary: "Resolve \"cross project token\" — symptom for the Auth surface."
---

Symptom

An operation against the Auth surface fails with an error matching "cross project token (symptom)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Auth API reference for the exact field set.

---

Symptom

An operation against the Buckets surface fails with an error matching "unexpected versioning state (reproduction)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Buckets API reference for the exact field set.

---

Symptom

An operation against the Webhooks surface fails with an error matching "callback not received (resolution)".

Resolution

Inspect the error envelope.Re-issue with corrected parameters.Confirm via the audit log.

See the Webhooks API reference for the exact field set.


---
title: Callback not received symptom (Webhooks)
url: https://example.com/troubleshooting/webhooks/callback-not-received-symptom/
type: troubleshooting
summary: "Resolve \"callback not received\" — symptom for the Webhooks surface."
